title: Chresta scapigera
taxonomy: Chresta scapigera is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The Asteraceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Chresta scapigera is part of the subfamily Mutisioideae. This classification places Chresta scapigera within the larger clade of flowering plants, more specifically within the angiosperms. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Chresta scapigera shares characteristics with other related species, such as daisy-like flowers and a similar growth habit. Further research may be needed to determine the exact relationships between Chresta scapigera and other species within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Chresta scapigera, also known as Chresta, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. Chresta has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Chresta include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chresta is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
